title: Expose bind mounts from one container to another in rootless mode
body:
/kind bug

**Can't expose fuse mounts from one container to another in rootless**

I got two test containers doing the same thing. The second container, however, is trying to mount the mounts from the first container using "--volumes-from" in rootless mode.

The folder is created inside the second container, but nothing inside.

```
podman run --rm -dt \
--name=test1 \
--privileged \
--volume ~/rclone:/config/rclone \
--volume ~/data/mount:/test1 \
--user 1000:1000 \
--volume /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro --volume /etc/group:/etc/group:ro \
--device /dev/fuse \
--security-opt apparmor=unconfined \
rclone/rclone \
mount remote-crypt:/test /test1 \
--allow-other \
--allow-non-empty

podman run --rm -dt \
--name=test2 \
--privileged \
--volume ~/rclone:/config/rclone \
--volume ~/data/mount:/test2 \
--user 1000:1000 \
--volume /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro --volume /etc/group:/etc/group:ro \
--device /dev/fuse \
--security-opt apparmor=unconfined \
rclone/rclone \
mount remote-crypt:/test /test2 \
--allow-other \
--allow-non-empty
```

I can see the test1 directory in the test2 container, but theres nothing inside.

I know exposing the mount to the host isn't possible in rootless mode: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10207

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.4
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
Built:        Thu Dec  9 15:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

Yes

comments.body:
you need to manually configure a mount on top of a parent of `~/data/mount` from the `podman unshare` environment and make it `shared`.  Then you have to bind mount that parent you made shared inside the container. e.g.

```
$ podman unshare mount --bind --make-shared ~/data ~/data
```
